original_hypothesis.title: StateHashPreImageLookup.set() Permissionless Write Enables Pre-Image Pollution for Upgrade
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: StateHashPreImageLookup.set() is fully permissionless - anyone can call it to store a pre-image for any state hash. While the hash validation (h == stateHash(executionState, inboxMaxCount)) prevents storing incorrect pre-images, the function uses inboxMaxCount != 0 as a sentinel for existence in get(). If a legitimate state hash happened to have inboxMaxCount == 0, it could never be stored/retrieved. Additionally, the permissionless nature means the lookup can be populated with valid pre-images at any time, potentially before the intended governance process sets them.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The BOLDUpgradeAction relies on PREIMAGE_LOOKUP.get() to fetch genesis state for the new rollup. If the pre-image data is manipulated or pre-populated with a different (but hash-valid) execution state, the upgrade could use incorrect genesis parameters.
deployment_assumptions: populateLookup.ts is called at the correct time with correct data before upgrade execution
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: BOLDUpgradeAction.sol StateHashPreImageLookup contract: set() is public with no access control. populateLookup.ts shows the off-chain process for populating the lookup.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function set(bytes32 h, ExecutionState calldata executionState, uint256 inboxMaxCount) public { require(h == stateHash(executionState, inboxMaxCount)); ... } - no access control

preconditions: Attacker can compute valid pre-image for the target state hash
State hash pre-image is unique (hash validation prevents storing wrong data, so attacker can only store the correct pre-image)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Public set() with no access control on upgrade-critical lookup contract
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: get() uses inboxMaxCount != 0 as existence sentinel; if valid state has inboxMaxCount == 0, upgrade breaks
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No onlyOwner or role-based access control on set()

analysis_questions: Can inboxMaxCount ever be 0 for a valid latest confirmed state?
Does the hash validation truly prevent any incorrect data from being stored?
Is pre-population by an attacker harmful if the data is hash-validated?
validation_requirements: Verify hash uniqueness guarantees one pre-image per state hash
Check if inboxMaxCount == 0 is possible for confirmed assertions
Test upgrade with pre-populated vs fresh lookup

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the latest confirmed assertion has inboxMaxCount == 0 (theoretically possible for genesis), the PREIMAGE_LOOKUP.get() call in createConfig would revert with 'Hash not yet set', permanently blocking the upgrade. While unlikely in practice (genesis creates at least one message), this sentinel value usage is a design weakness.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All rollup stakeholders waiting for upgrade
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Deploy new BOLDUpgradeAction without sentinel check

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for edge case of inboxMaxCount == 0 in StateHashPreImageLookup

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by checking latestConfirmed state pre-upgrade

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
